title: CompositeChannelFactory routes IChannel creation by config.type to registered sub-factories.
content:
## Claim
`CompositeChannelFactory` implements `IChannelFactory` but dispatches `instance()` calls to registered sub-factories keyed by `config.type` (e.g., `"gql"`, `"swarm"`).

## Evidence
The source defines a `factories: Map<string, IChannelFactory>` with `register(type, factory)`. On `instance()`, it looks up `config.type` and delegates, throwing if the type is unregistered.

```typescript
const factory = this.factories.get(config.type);
if (!factory) throw new Error(`Unknown channel type "${config.type}"...`);
return factory.instance(remoteId, remoteName, config, cursorStorage, collectionId, filter, operationIndex);
```

## Why this pattern
The reactor exposes only one `IChannelFactory` slot per instance. Without the composite, adding Swarm would require monkey-patching or forking the reactor. The composite is a clean strategy/registry pattern that leaves the reactor interface untouched.

## Source
`adapter/src/channel/composite-factory.ts`; Swarm Channel Architecture, "CompositeChannelFactory" section.
description: The reactor accepts one IChannelFactory. A composite implementation dispatches to named sub-factories so GQL and Swarm channels can coexist.
